# Day 43 — CloudTrail Log Analysis
 
**Date:** 2026-06-24
**Note:** CloudTrail enabled on AWS Free Tier account. Logs generated by creating and deleting test resources. Analysis below based on actual log output.
 
---
 
## What CloudTrail Logs Look Like
 
CloudTrail stores logs as JSON files in an S3 bucket. Each file contains an array of events. Each event is a JSON object representing one API call.
 
### Sample Event — IAM User Created
 
```json
{
  "eventVersion": "1.08",
  "userIdentity": {
    "type": "IAMUser",
    "principalId": "AIDIODR4TAW7CSEXAMPLE",
    "arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/admin",
    "accountId": "123456789012",
    "userName": "admin"
  },
  "eventTime": "2026-06-24T14:23:01Z",
  "eventSource": "iam.amazonaws.com",
  "eventName": "CreateUser",
  "awsRegion": "us-east-1",
  "sourceIPAddress": "197.210.65.42",
  "userAgent": "AWS Internal",
  "requestParameters": {
    "userName": "testuser"
  },
  "responseElements": {
    "user": {
      "path": "/",
      "userName": "testuser",
      "userId": "AIDIODR4TAW7CSEXAMPLE2",
      "arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/testuser",
      "createDate": "Jun 24, 2026 2:23:01 PM"
    }
  }
}
```
